free_text: Full paper of contribution to Symposium on Neutron
Cross Sections from 10-50 MeV, held at Upton,
Measurement of the angle-integrated secondary neutron
spectra from interaction of 14 MeV neutrons with
Using a small low-mass titanium-tritium target.
Hollow cylinders of about 6 cm3 with natural isotopic
calibration between 0.25 and 6 MeV neutron energy.
The experimental results are compared with existing
measurements and with statistical model calculations
scattered in sample to 14.7 MeV peak.
The errors given were obtained by quadratic
addition of the statistical errors estimated 1 sigma
errors for all identified sources of systematic errors
and thus correspond to effective 1 sigma errors. The
following contributions are included in the error
- the total statistical error including the error
- the uncertainty in the form of the Cf-252
- the error in the absorption correction factor for
the error for the multiple scattering correctio
- an uncertainty of 1.5% was assumed for the
normalization factors used in the background
a 3% efficiency error for the 14.7 MeV
